info.title: OpenUI MCP Compatibility Surface
info.summary: Compatibility bridge for the local stdio MCP builder surface.
info.description: Contract-oriented HTTP projection of the local stdio MCP interface for compatibility, bridge consumers, and breaking-change review. This document mirrors the repo-side builder surface boundary exposed through services/mcp-server/src/public/index.ts. It is not the primary contract for the separate self-hosted Hosted API or public SDK product lines, and it does not imply a remote-write control plane.

servers.description: Compatibility projection only; runtime transport remains stdio and the primary builder surface remains local MCP.

tags.name: Compatibility Bridge
tags.description: Secondary bridge for contract review and adapter compatibility. Read this after the local stdio MCP surface, not instead of it.

x-openui-builder-surface-order.bestFor: Starting a real MCP-native prompt-to-workspace integration from the canonical local runtime.
x-openui-builder-surface-order.readWhen: Read this first when the question is how to execute OpenUI from Codex, Claude Code, or another MCP client.
x-openui-builder-surface-order.notFor: Hosted API expectations, remote mutation assumptions, or schema-only review without the MCP runtime.
x-openui-builder-surface-order.description: Primary builder surface for Codex, Claude Code, and other MCP clients.

x-openui-builder-surface-order.bestFor: Compatibility review, adapter shaping, and HTTP-facing contract inspection after the MCP path is clear.
x-openui-builder-surface-order.readWhen: Read this second when you need schema-oriented inspection instead of the primary runtime entrypoint.
x-openui-builder-surface-order.notFor: Treating the compatibility bridge itself as the primary hosted API, SDK, or remote-write builder control-plane contract.
x-openui-builder-surface-order.description: Secondary bridge for contract review and adapter compatibility; the self-hosted Hosted API and public SDK live on separate product-line contracts.

x-openui-builder-surface-order.bestFor: Repo-local plus GitHub-connected readiness checks and non-mutating handoff packets for maintainers.
x-openui-builder-surface-order.readWhen: Read this third when the question becomes whether the repo is ready to move toward PR or checks review.
x-openui-builder-surface-order.notFor: Replacing the MCP runtime, acting like a public workflow service, or mutating remote state.
x-openui-builder-surface-order.description: Maintainer-facing read-only workflow packet that stays separate from remote mutation.

x-openui-builder-dispatcher.description: Repo-local dispatcher and formal entry helper for the current builder surface order.

x-openui-builder-surface-guide.audience: Zero-context builders evaluating or integrating OpenUI MCP Studio from the repo itself.
x-openui-builder-surface-guide.startWith: Begin with the local stdio MCP surface because it is the canonical runtime entrypoint and the rest of the builder layer only explains or supports that runtime.
x-openui-builder-surface-guide.openapiWhen: Read the OpenAPI bridge after the MCP-first flow is clear and you need compatibility review, adapter shaping, or breaking-change inspection.
x-openui-builder-surface-guide.workflowPacketWhen: Read the workflow packet when you are in maintainer/operator mode and need repo-local plus GitHub-connected readiness without remote mutation.
x-openui-builder-surface-guide.skillsStarterWhen: Open examples/skills only after the first three surfaces are clear and you need a starter contract for a future skill-shaped integration.
notFor: Treating the builder-order guide as the primary SDK or hosted API product contract
Claiming the SDK or self-hosted Hosted API replaces local stdio MCP in the builder order

x-openui-later-lanes.description: Registry publication for package surfaces remains operator-owned.
Managed hosted deployment remains later/operator-owned even though the self-hosted Hosted API runtime is current.
Remote mutation remains out of scope for the current builder-facing contract.
Plugin, marketplace, and formal skills packaging stay in later productization lanes.

get.description: List tool metadata available through the compatibility bridge. Read this as a projection of the local stdio MCP registry, not as evidence of a separate hosted control plane.

post.summary: Invoke MCP tool via compatibility bridge
post.description: Invoke one MCP tool through a compatibility adapter. The canonical runtime remains the local stdio MCP server, and response envelopes may vary by adapter.

ToolArguments.description: Bridge-projected tool arguments. Shape depends on the selected MCP tool input schema.

McpToolDescriptor.description: Compatibility projection of tool metadata. Concrete adapter payloads may include additional fields.

McpToolListResponse.description: Compatibility bridge response for tool discovery. The payload stays intentionally bridge-oriented and does not imply a hosted REST-native SDK contract.

McpToolCallResponse.description: Compatibility projection of an MCP tool result. Content blocks and extra metadata can vary by adapter.
